findings.summary: Wien bridge RC filters detected at 145 MHz and 7.2 MHz — likely false positives since Wien bridge operates at audio frequencies. Op-amp classified as comparator_or_open_loop instead of oscillator.

correct.description: OP97 detected as op-amp (present on board)

incorrect.description: R3/C7 at 145 MHz and R9/C7 at 7.2 MHz — these cutoff frequencies are implausibly high for a Wien bridge oscillator. May be picking up unrelated R-C pairs.

missed.description: Wien bridge oscillator topology not detected — the positive-feedback loop with frequency-setting RC network is the key feature of this board but has no detector

suggestions: Consider adding oscillator detection (Wien bridge, phase-shift, Colpitts, etc.) as a signal type. The positive feedback around op-amp + matched RC network is a recognizable pattern.

findings.summary: Differential pair detector matches any X+/X- net name pattern, producing FPs on V+/V- power rails and IN+/IN- opamp inputs. ESD detector then flags opamps as ESD devices.

incorrect.description: IN+/IN- opamp input labels detected as differential pair; V+/V- power rails detected as differential pair
OP97 opamp (U1) flagged as ESD protection device on the IN+/IN- "differential pair"

missed.description: Wien bridge oscillator topology not detected
C7 (22pF) capacitive negative feedback from OUT to IN- not detected — only resistive feedback recognized

suggestions: Exclude power nets (V+/V-, VCC/VEE) from diff pair detection. Verify diff pair ICs are actually ESD/protection devices via lib_id. Recognize capacitive feedback paths.

findings.summary: Both RC filter detections (R3/C7 at 145 MHz, R9/C7 at 7.2 MHz) are false positives — R3 is output termination, R9 is Wien bridge positive feedback, C7 is negative feedback cap. They share the OUT node but serve different functions.

incorrect.description: R3(49.9ohm)/C7(22pF) at 145 MHz — R3 is output series termination, C7 is negative feedback cap. Not an RC filter pair.
R9(1k)/C7(22pF) at 7.2 MHz — R9 is Wien bridge positive feedback, C7 is negative feedback. Not an RC filter pair.

suggestions: RC filter detector should verify R and C form an actual signal path (series or shunt) rather than just sharing a node
